one_liner: conformal risk control FNR bound for medical segmentation
problem: Medical segmentation FNR has no formal upper bound in deployment; calibration is ECE point estimate with no coverage guarantee.
approach: 1.Apply Angelopoulos 2024 CRC to define FNR loss on segmentation output. 2.Verify coverage on BraTS2021 and Camelyon16. 3.Compare temperature scaling and MC-Dropout FNR.
why_new: CRC original only handles NLP or classification. MICCAI 2025 morphological prediction sets does not target FNR. Angle: control FNR not coverage rate.
source_method: conformal risk control (Angelopoulos et al. ICLR 2024)

one_liner: LLM verbalized uncertainty to medical VLM calibration check
problem: Med-VLM outputs hedges but nobody checked whether confidence words map to calibrated numeric probabilities.
approach: 1.Extract Med-VLM outputs on CheXpert/HAM10000 and match confidence tokens. 2.Build word-probability mapping compute ECE. 3.Test if prompt engineering aligns linguistic uncertainty with probabilities.
why_new: Verbalized uncertainty (Kadavath 2022, Tian 2023) is mature in NLP but absent in medical VLM; existing Med-VLM calibration only looks at logit layer not language layer.

one_liner: Autonomous-driving epistemic/aleatoric OOD decomposition to cross-site chest X-ray failure diagnosis
problem: When chest X-ray models fail across centers clinicians cannot distinguish inherent data ambiguity from unseen scanner type; treatment strategies differ entirely.
approach: 1.Train DUQ/SNGP on NIH-CXR14 evaluate on VinDr-CXR. 2.Decompose uncertainty components visualize per-anatomical-region. 3.Verify high-epistemic samples correlate with cross-center AUC drop.
why_new: Epistemic/aleatoric decomposition (Kendall and Gal 2017, Postels 2020) standard in autonomous driving; medical imaging almost exclusively uses blended MC-Dropout. Target: clinically interpretable decomposition report not SOTA AUC.
source_method: autodrive epistemic/aleatoric decomposition (Kendall and Gal 2017; Postels 2020 DUQ; Liu 2020 SNGP)

one_liner: Meteorological ensemble spread-skill calibration to multi-annotator medical label uncertainty
problem: STAPLE/MV discards annotator disagreement as noise but disagreement carries clinical uncertainty signal. Meteorological spread-vs-RMSE calibration has never been applied to multi-rater medical scenarios.
approach: 1.Compute inter-annotator spread on BraTS 3-rater subset. 2.Apply spread-skill analysis verify spread predicts actual segmentation error. 3.Design human-in-loop referral policy triggered by poor spread-skill calibration.
why_new: Meteorological spread-skill (Gneiting 2007, Leutbecher 2019) completely unknown in medical annotation; STAPLE never treats spread as calibration signal.
source_method: meteorological spread-skill (Gneiting et al. 2007; Leutbecher and Palmer 2019)
